titleEn: Indeed We Are All Perishing
People in Their Heedlessness
My God, Do Not Punish Me
O Servants of God, What Have You with This World
The World Is an Abode of Tribulation
Cease Your Blame
Pour Me Wine
O Moon I Beheld
Bid Farewell to the Ruins
When I Die, Bury Me
A pure wine laughing in its cup
O one who places the sandgrouse eggs
O you who broke your promise
I was once patient and steadfast
She of the rosy, rose-red cheek
Two lovers whose cheeks intertwined
I shall thank memory for what it does for me

titleAr: قالوا : تنسك بعد الحج ، قلت لهم :
titleEn: They said become pious after the pilgrimage
The veil of night is now lifted
Rejection made me taste my own mismanagement
Seize your youth before grey hair and disgrace

titleAr: أبحت حريم الكأس إذ كنت مثريا ،
titleEn: I opened the sanctum of the cup when I was wealthy
Abandoning the morning drink is a sign of decline

titleAr: إني صرفت الهوى إلى قمر إني صرفت الهوى إلى قمر
titleEn: I have turned my passion toward a moon

titleAr: يا ذا الذي عن جنان ظل يخبرني ،
titleEn: O you who has been telling me about Jinan
A full-breasted maid of the palace servants

titleAr: إلى الله أشكو حب من جل نيله
titleEn: To God I complain of love for one hard to attain

titleAr: قنعت ، إذ نلت من أحبابي النظرا
titleEn: I was content when I gained but a glance from my beloved
Say to Ibrahim a taunting word

titleAr: من يزدري الكبش في الدنيا ويحقره
titleEn: Whoever scorns the ram in this world
A young man who adorns his loaf with earrings

titleAr: رأيت قدور الناس سودا من الصلى ،
titleEn: I saw people's cooking pots blackened by fire
Return them, O Muhammad ibn Zuhayr
I see myself living among the living while most of me is dead

titleAr: طوى الموت ما بيني وبين محمد ،
titleEn: Death has folded away what lay between me and Muhammad
With what shall I satirize you? I do not know!
I was devoted to riding the nag until
Be patient with the bitter events of time

titleAr: أيا من ليس لي منه مجير ،
titleEn: O You from Whom I have no refuge
Will you not visit the graves one morning
O Children of Weakness and Folly
Counsel to Oneself on Patience
The Seeker of God Shall Triumph
Going Out with Keen Grey Falcons
The Falcon Surpasses the Sparrowhawk
The Abandoned Tavern of Wine Companions
Time Gnawed Me and Love Struck Me Down
Despair of People's Loyalty

titleAr: ألا ليت شعري هكذا أنت للناس
titleEn: Are You Like This with Everyone?
Paper Merchants Who Cannot Spare a Sheet
The Good Men Are Gone, Leaving Wretches
Describing a Keen Hunting Hound
How Are You, O Muhammad ibn Quraysh?
A Languid Beloved Who Reproaches the Poet
True Praise for the Caliph al-Amin
A Message of Harsh Reproach for Hamdan
Gazelles of the Royal Court
O You Who Turn Away, My Soul Is Your Ransom
When You Were Beardless Your Beauty Shone
The Yolk Is Gone and Only the Membrane Remains
O Beloved Whose Illness Worsened My Heart
O You Who Embody Pure Beauty
Drink Without Restraint, God Forgives
Love Broke My Vitality
A Beautiful Youth by the Nile
I Traded Vitality for Brokenness Through Love
The Hunting Dog Tears Through Fresh Prey
A Fierce Dog Prepared for the Hunt

titleAr: ما مثل هذا اليوم في طيبه
titleEn: No Day Finer Than This for Wine
I Have Abandoned Folly on the Caliph's Command
Pour Me Seven in Succession
Would That the Omen-Reader's Rebuke Were Here
Three Who Ruled Kings, Each Incomparable
Had My Fortune Not Been Ill
I Woke the Hungriest of All God's Creation
Tell Isma'il of the Mole

titleAr: ما رعى الدهر آل برمك حقا
titleEn: Time Did Not Honor the Barmakids
My Father as Ransom for One Who Visited Me
Pour for Me and Pour for Dhafafah
Obey the Caliph, Defy the Musician

titleAr: لو كان حي وائلا من التلف
titleEn: If Any Living Thing Could Escape Perdition

titleAr: شعر ميت أتاك في لفظ حي
titleEn: Dead Poetry Came to You in Living Words
A Blamer Rebuked Me, Bringing Innovation
O Blamer of the Lover, You Are the One
I Have Died Save for the Last Breath
O You Who Direct My Words to Their Ugliest
My Blamer in Wine, I Will Not Please You
O Month, How Long Will You Last?
When Separation Is Mentioned, He Weeps
If the One You Love Loved You in Return
You Threatened Me with Death Without Cause
The Water of Youth Flows in Your Cheeks
Our God, How Just You Are
My Friends, By God, Do Not Dig
Youth Was the Mount of Ignorance
By Your Life, al-Amin Muhammad Was Not Absent

titleAr: يا ربع شغلك إني عنك في شغل
titleEn: O Ruined Encampment, I Have My Own Concern
O Lady of the Beautiful Face

titleAr: أأسلمتني يا جعفر بن أبي الفضل
titleEn: Have You Abandoned Me, O Ja'far Son of Abu al-Fadl
Pour the Wine Back to Me in the Cup

titleAr: يا رب ليل بت في نعمة
titleEn: O Lord, Many a Night I Spent in Bliss
Wanderers Who Lost Their Way
The Wretch Wanders Over the Ruined Dwellings
My Eyes Refuse to Sleep Without You

titleAr: كان حلما ما كنت آمل فيكم
titleEn: What I Hoped From You Was a Dream

titleAr: يا رب إن عظمت ذنوبي كثرة
titleEn: O Lord, Though My Sins Are Great in Number
Leave Your Side to the Archer
My Heart Is Patient and My Tongue Is Silent
I Die and You Don't Know, Though You Killed Me
One Whose Glance Complies Yet Whose Tongue Is Chaste

titleAr: أعلم أن لا خير لي عندكم
titleEn: I Know There Is No Good for Me With You
Lovelier Than the Description of Ruined Traces
As for the Dwellings, They Seldom Tarried There
We Withdrew from People When They Grew Aware
My Beloved Is Unjust to Me and Withholding
One Whose Temples Are Curled Like a Scorpion's Tail
Alert of Glance Beneath Drowsy Eyelids
Your Love, O Ahmad, Has Wasted Me

titleAr: لم أزل أخلع في الحب الرسن
titleEn: I Have Never Ceased Casting Off Love's Halter
People Have Prepared for the Festival
He Made Estrangement Apparent After Union
O Night I Spent Being Given to Drink
O You Who Reproach Me Over Wine
I Was Alone With Wine, Whispering to It
O Night I Spent in Its Darknesses
No Youth Has Perfected Pleasures
Leave Me From the Dwelling — Let Me Weep Over It
Fadl Has Grown Openly Arrogant

titleAr: لا تفرغ النفس من شغل بدنياها
titleEn: The Soul Never Empties of Its Worldly Preoccupations
My Youth Has Passed and I Have Abandoned Pleasures

titleAr: كم ليلة قد بت ألهو بها
titleEn: How Many a Night I Spent in Pleasure
I Set Out at Dawn While Darkness Still Lingers

titleAr: من يك من حبيك خلوا ، فما
titleEn: Though Others Be Free of Your Love
O Fadl, You Have Entrusted Me with Wisdom
What al-Ghalibi Has Met — What a Fate!
Something in Me Dies Every Day
The Conquest of Amorium — The Sword Is More Truthful
In Praise of al-Mu'tasim
My Heart Was Moved
On Courage and Battle
From the Hamasa — On Dignity
O Resting Place of the Swift She-Camel
Your Manner Betrays Your Secret Counsel, O Blamer
Do You Not See How Truthful the Rain-Stars Are?
Enough! Return! You Have Gone Too Far in Excess

titleAr: أجل أيها الربع الذي خف آهله
titleEn: Indeed, O Dwelling Whose People Have Departed
The Sword Is More Truthful in Its Tidings Than Books
Through Muhammad, Time Became Praiseworthy

titleAr: لو أن دهرا رد رجع جواب
titleEn: If Only Fate Would Return an Answer

titleAr: قل لابن طوق رحى سعد إذا خبطت
titleEn: Say to Ibn Tawq: The Millstone of Sa'd
A Journey Drew Near, and Fortune Withdraws and Returns
How Beautiful Are the Days of al-Aqiq!
I Settled My Saddle in the Approaching Pasture
I Saw Nothing but the Venom of Toil
Al-Hasan ibn Wahb

titleAr: لا نالك العثر من دهر ولا زلل
titleEn: May Stumbling and Slipping Never Befall You
Be Patient, O My Soul

titleAr: أبدت أسى أن رأتني مخلس القصب
titleEn: She Showed Grief When She Saw My Temples Gray

titleAr: أما أبو بشر فقد أضحى الورى
titleEn: As for Abu Bishr, All People Have Become
The Clouds, Between Evening and Morning Draughts
What a Pasture for the Eye, What a Valley of Love Poetry!
How Strange That Your Face Is Turned Away
The Gazes of al-Hasan ibn Wahb Are the Sweetest
The Emir Has Tested You in His Affairs

titleAr: لا خير في قربى بغير مودة
titleEn: No Good in Kinship Without Affection
O Our Days! You Were Nothing But Gifts
O My Shield, My Support, and My Sustainer!
Praise Be! — From a Rain-Star Worthy of Praise
I Follow My Impulses — I Am Not Ruled by My Reproacher
I Am Bishr — I Have Opened a Door
The Hand of Complaint Has Come to You by Post
Among the Ruins' Qualities Is Silence
The Day of Parting, How Long You Were Made
The Veil Has Been Lifted, So Kindle or Extinguish
Indeed a Letter Came to Me From You
Patience Was Taken From Him the Day They Departed

titleAr: أأطلال هند ساء ما اعتضت من هند
titleEn: O Ruins of Hind, How Badly You Replaced Hind

titleAr: لقد أخذت من دار ماوية الحقب
titleEn: The Ages Have Taken Their Toll on Mawiyya's Dwelling
Stop Your Worrying, For I Have Turned Against You
Stop and Renew Your Covenant with the Old Dwelling Places
On Such Ruins and Playgrounds
The Kingdom Became Magnificent in Its Sanctuaries and Dwellings
Death Has Rolled Me Up While I Was Lost in Pleasure
O Arrow of the Lightning That Flashed Forth

titleAr: قل للأمير قد نال ما طلبا
titleEn: Say to the Prince: He Has Attained What He Sought
It Would Be Easy for Us If We Were the Speakers and You the Doer
I Looked and Saw: There Is No Correctness for the Rational

titleAr: قد نابت الجزع من أروية النوب
titleEn: Misfortunes Have Befallen the Acacia Valley from the Wild Goat's Lair

titleAr: متى أنت عن ذهلية الحي ذاهل
titleEn: When Will You Forget the Dhahliyya Woman of the Tribe
Now That You Have Attached Me to the Cavalcade
The Affairs of Polytheism Ended in the Worst Ending

titleAr: إن بكاء في الدار من أربه
titleEn: If There Is Weeping in the House Among Its Interests

titleAr: جعلت فداك أنت من لا ندله
titleEn: May I Be Your Ransom — You Are the One We Do Not Guide
I Testify That You Have Worn, O Abu Said
Have All the Tribes of Adnan Ever Assembled
May God's Peace Be as Countless as the Sand of Khubat
Ruins He Visited and Said Peace Upon You
A Gentle Rain, Easy-Tempered, Pouring
O Arama, You Were the Haunt of Every Gazelle

titleAr: لا عيش أو يتحامى جسمك الصب
titleEn: No Life Until Your Lovesick Body Is Protected

titleAr: أصغى إلى البين مغترا فلا جرما
titleEn: He Listened to Separation Carelessly, So Inevitably
I Had a Hope That Has Now Passed
O Nursery of Elegance, Branch of Noble Lineage

titleAr: ألم يأن أن تروى الظماء الحوائم
titleEn: Has It Not Come Time for the Thirsting to Drink
Strike the Reproacher Dead with This Cup's Intoxication

titleAr: أبا جعفر أضحى بك الظن ممرعا
titleEn: Abu Ja'far, Hope Has Grown Verdant Through You

titleAr: سلم على الربع من سلمى بذي سلم
titleEn: Offer Peace to the Encampment of Salma at Dhi Salam

titleAr: لن يبق للصيف لا رسم ولا طلل
titleEn: Summer Will Leave No Trace or Ancient Ruin
We Ask Her: To Which Homelands Has She Gone
One Land Is Parched and Another Pours with Rain
Perhaps You Will Remember the Ancient Ruins
I Say to the Seeker of Generosity at Malik's Door
What Ails the Tears Seeking Every Direction
Struck Down by Passion, Grief Visits Him Each Morning
Halt at the Worn Ruins, Tarrying There
I Am in the Protection of the Noble, Secure
Day of Separation, You Were Created Immense
The Turning of Separation Is Not Long in Stay
Do You Claim the Encampment Is Not Orphaned
This Is the Letter of a Youth Whose Ambitions Are High
Abu Sa'id, My Praise Is Not to Be Doubted
Tell the Prince You Have Bestowed Favors upon Me
When Has My Hearing Been a Prize for Blamers
This Covenant, Were You to Know, Is a Reproach

titleAr: أهد الدموع إلى دار وما صحها
titleEn: Guide the Tears to a Dwelling Though It Cannot Be Cured
May a Homeland Draw Them Near, Perhaps the Days Will Relent

titleAr: سقى الله من اهوى على بعد نائه
titleEn: May God Water the One I Love Despite Distance
The Exile of Separation Has Become Fortunate Through Su'ad

titleAr: قل للأمير أبي سعيد ذي الندى
titleEn: Tell the Prince Abu Sa'id, Lord of Generosity
I Have Exhausted in Your Presence the Meanings of Complaint
May Clouds Water the Memory of the Sanctuary
O Abu Said, Blessings Meet at Your Hands
Will My Lord Strip Me of Wealth
I Scattered Tears Like Unstrung Pearls
I Remembered You Until I Nearly Forgot You
Did You See What Cheeks and Temples Appeared
O Malik, Grief Is But a Dreamer's Dream
Alone in Beauty, Empty of Love
O Ahmad, the Envious Are Multitudes

titleAr: يا ربع لو ربعوا على ابن هموم
titleEn: O Abode, If They Had Halted for a Grief-Stricken Man

titleAr: هي فرقة من صاحب لك ماجد
titleEn: It Is Parting from a Noble Companion
Were It Not for Abu Ya'qub's Resolve
Finding Comfort in Nothing When You Are Absent
Ruins of the Gathering, You Have Faded Honorably
O Sons of Hamid Allah, Your Excellence
You Extinguished the Fire of Your Love in My Heart
What Has Befallen the Sand-Dune of the Sacred Ground
Her Clan and Intimates Blamed Her
He Who Nurtures Grief in Hearts
People at Habina'a Say, Having Seen
May Clouds Water That Land Fully and Lightly

titleAr: لأشكرنك إن لم أوت من أجلي
titleEn: I Shall Thank You Even If I Receive Nothing

titleAr: إلياس كن في ضمان الله والذمم
titleEn: O Ilyas, Be Under God's Protection
O My Two Companions, Both of Whom Answer
You Have Watered the Thirsty and Barren Plain
Would That the Gazelles Had Told Him News
My Phantom Met Him in Slumber and He Avoided It

titleAr: يا بعد غاية العين إن بعدوا
titleEn: O How Far the Eye's Reach When They Depart
You Were Detained and for Your Sake the Rains Were Held

titleAr: لئن جحدتك ما لاقيت فيك لقد
titleEn: If I Denied What I Suffered in You

titleAr: سرت تستجير الدمع خوف نوى غد
titleEn: She Walked Seeking Refuge in Tears from Tomorrow's Separation
May Thunderclouds Water Their Ruins
We Withheld Our Gazes Fearing They Would Melt
I Shall Thank the Sons of Wahb for Their Gift

titleAr: ما اليوم أول توديع ولا الثاني
titleEn: Today Is Not the First Farewell nor the Second

titleAr: تجرع أسى قد أقفر الجرع الفرد
titleEn: Drink the Grief of the Solitary Desolate Sandy Plain

titleAr: ألقت على غاربي حبل امرئ عان
titleEn: Fate Cast Upon My Shoulders the Rope of a Captive Man
My Tears Grew Beautiful and My Weeping Grew Sweet
I Sacrifice Myself for You, Abdullah Is Dear to Me
May God Grant You Bodily Health in Succession
My Gaze at You Signals to Me

titleAr: أبا القاسم المحمود ، إن ذكر الحمد
titleEn: O Abu al-Qasim the Praised, If Praise Is Mentioned
The Battle Has Subdued al-Badhh and It Lies Buried
A Sun of Cloudy Skies Rose from a Willow Branch

titleAr: يا دار دار عليك إرهام الندى
titleEn: O Ruin May the Gentle Rains Drizzle Upon You
By My Father the Dwelling-Places Are Truly Full of Grief

titleAr: يا أيها السائلي عن عرصة الجود
titleEn: O You Who Ask About the Arena of Generosity
The Emir Is the Refuge of the Offender and the Criminal
I Am Dying and If I Die I Die from My Love
If You Wish You May Follow Kindness with Kindness
A Moon That Smiled Revealing Fresh Pearls
The Four Encampments of the Tender Ones Have Been Effaced
I See You Disdain My Devotion to the Ruins
I Have a Beloved Whose Advice I Disobeyed
You Would Have Aspired to Lightning and Thunder

titleAr: أبا قدامة قد قدمت لي قدما
titleEn: O Abu Qudama You Have Established a Precedent for Me
May the Eyes of the Rain-Clouds Water Your Land from Me

titleAr: قال الوشاة بدا في الخد عارضه
titleEn: The Talebearers Said His Beard Has Appeared on His Cheek
I Think Her Tears Are the Course of the Singular Pearl
A Woman Said Abd al-Aziz Has Performed the Pilgrimage
Grant My Eye a Share in Sleep
O Namesake of the Prophet Who Prays

titleAr: يقول في قومس صحبي وقد أخذت
titleEn: My Companions Ask in Qumas
May Joy Be Yours, Noble Scion
Your Tears Have Given Their Utmost
Woe to the Grieved from the Carefree
By the Rose Upon His Cheek
A Caller Summoned with a Guide's Tongue
Announce the Death to All the Living
He Turned Away, Unexpectedly
Eyelids of the Supple Willow Branch
O Muhammad ibn Said, Reserve Your Grief
I Am in Pain and Severe Grief
A Singing Bird Awakened Your Longing

titleAr: هو الدهر لا يشوي وهن المصائب
titleEn: Time Does Not Waste the Weak of Calamities
One Who Enchants with His Glance and Cheek
The Prince Has Taken the Place of Every Helper
A Deaf Fortune Heedless of Reproach

titleAr: رأيت في النوم أن الصلح قد فسدا
titleEn: I Saw in a Dream the Peace Was Broken
Nawar Among Her Companions
What Generosity Between the Earth and Grain
You Have Brought Me Beyond the Peak of Grief
Tell the Generous Prince
Weeping Is My Eye's Habit, Grief Is Mine

titleAr: وفى البكا بالعهد اذ لم يكن
titleEn: Weeping Kept the Covenant
Muhammad, I Would Be Blameworthy Without You

titleAr: جفوف البلى أسرعت في الغصن الرطب
titleEn: The Drying of Decay Hastened Upon the Wet Branch
Separation Has Snatched Ahmad ibn Yazid

titleAr: لا أنت أنت ولا الديار ديار
titleEn: You Are No Longer You, the Abodes No Longer Abodes
Repeat the Lamentation, O Wailing Woman
I Shall Never Eat Apples
The Season Grows Tender, Spring Arrives
Elegy for Khalid, Lament for Generosity
One Desire I Have in This World
Truth Is Radiant, Swords Are Blazing

titleAr: لو صحح الدمع لي أو ناصح الكمد
titleEn: Had Tears Been True or Grief Sincere
Unique in Beauty, Offspring of Light
My Night Passes Yet Never Ends
Let Calamity Be Grave, the Matter Weighty
O Sick One, Your Breast Is Filled with Fire

titleAr: شجا في الحشى ترداده ليس يفتر
titleEn: A Grief Repeating in My Breast Unceasing
Take Consolation, for None Endure
A Wine Whose Star Blazes
O Ahmad, Enviers Are Many
Sufficient Against All Time's Trials

titleAr: سهرت فيك فلم أجحد يد السهر
titleEn: I Kept Vigil for You, Not Denying Sleeplessness

titleAr: يا وارث الملك إن الملك محتبس
titleEn: O Heir to Kingship, Reign Is Reserved for You

titleAr: يا سمي النبي في سورة الج
titleEn: O Namesake of the Prophet in the Surah of Jinn

titleAr: أصم بك الناعي وإن كان أسمعا
titleEn: The Death-Announcer Has Deafened Me
Is There Any Trace Left of Their Abodes
My Father's Life and More: An Elegy for Muhammad
Heavy of Hip and Slender of Waist
She Said, Women's Wiles Are Like the Mute
A Grief That Grips the Heart and Entrails

titleAr: ما في وقوفك ساعة من باس
titleEn: No Harm in Pausing an Hour at the Ruins
Days Have Always Told the Questioner
O You by Whom Glory Itself Glories
O Time, Enough of You — My Reproach Avails Little
Your Hands Have Covered Me in My Grave

titleAr: يا هذه أقصري ما هذه بشر
titleEn: O Woman, Relent — These Are No Mortals
Let Not Enemies Gloat at Death

titleAr: من أين لي صبر على الهجر
titleEn: Where Can I Find Patience
Reviving a Stolen Heart
Muhammad's Death Recalls Muhammad
Leaning Like a Blooming Branch
Their Dwelling Once Fresh Now Worn

titleAr: لا تعذلي جارتي إنى لك العذل
titleEn: Do Not Blame Me, Neighbour
I Thank Her Before Her Union
Asma Lowered Him the Sun's Rope
We Slept but Time Did Not

titleAr: قد صنف الحسن في خديك جوهره
titleEn: Beauty Arranged Its Jewels in Your Cheeks
Do the Proud of Bakr Boast, O Weakling
Muhammad ibn Humaid, His Bones Now Worn
Your Teeth Like Hail and Lightning
God Have Mercy on Ja'far
This Is Your Love and Its Traces
The Gazelle of the Dunes, But For Her Legs

titleAr: اليوم أدرج زيد الخيل في كفن
titleEn: Today Zayd al-Khayl Is Wrapped in a Shroud
The Day of Parting Is a Grim Day
Your People Departed Raised and Dismantled
Have You Not Seen Me Leave My Soul to Its Affairs
Leave Me to Drink Passion, O Wine-Drinker
Tears Replaced the Flash of Lightning
The Hand of Generosity Left Without Fingers
O Fawn Fashioned from the Sun
Restlessness Robs the Eye of Sleep

titleAr: إني أظن البلى لو كان يفهمه
titleEn: Decay Would Spare Such Beauty
O You Cloaked in the Sun's Garment
Were It Not for the Departing Companions
My Soul Is Muhammad's Ransom
O You Who Wear Grace's Garment Unknowingly
Spare Your Tears for My Resolve

titleAr: بيت قلبي من هواك على الطوى
titleEn: My Heart's House Starves for Your Love
May My Soul Ransom This Beloved

titleAr: قد كسانا من كسوة الصيف خرق
titleEn: A Nobleman Clothed Us in Summer Robes
I Spent the Night at War with Sleep
Abu Ali and His Pasturing Ground
Distance Separated Him from His Kin

titleAr: غدا يتناءى صاحب كان لي انسا
titleEn: Tomorrow a Dear Companion Parts
Here Is the Station of the Grief-Stricken
Your Servant Prays with Open Palms

titleAr: أما الرسوم فقد أذكرن ما سلفا
titleEn: The Ruins Have Recalled What Passed
Breath Urging Breath
Their Ruins Robbed of Graceful Gazelles
He Stole a Glance in Bewilderment
Tell Ibrahim and Al-Fadl
I Endured with Unconquered Patience
By Him Who Gave You Strength and Power
A Sick Man Wept at Sick Ruins
Your Sincere Servant Answers Your Call

titleAr: أغنيت عني غناء الماء في الشرق
titleEn: You Enriched Me as Water Enriches the Thirsty

titleAr: لي لا كان من هواك خلاص
titleEn: There Is No Escape from Your Love

titleAr: قد شرد الصبح هذا الليل عن أفقه
titleEn: Dawn Has Chased Night from the Horizon
He Who Robs My Eyes of Sleep
Fate's Turns Come from Your Separation
Perfumed with Musk on His Cheeks
O Lightning Visiting the Abraq Abode
My Father Ransoms Him, Beautiful as He Is
Grant My Eyes a Share of Sleep
Let Me Pour My Tears for You
Beauty of Wondrous Form Smiling Like the Moon
Never Have We Known Such Yearning
Burning Regrets and Tender Yearning
O Lightning, Rest at the Summit of al-Buraq
Rain Drew Near and Lightning Poured Down

titleAr: يا قضيبا لا يدانيه من الإنس قضيب
titleEn: O Branch No Human Branch Can Match

titleAr: لم أر شيئا من الفراق اذا
titleEn: Nothing in Parting Is Harder Than Farewell

titleAr: إن يكن في الأرض شيء حسن
titleEn: If Anything on Earth Is Beautiful
The Caravan Has Made Me Their Tale
She Scratched Me with Her Palm
My Flowing Tears Water Their Dwelling

titleAr: تبدلت الفا إذ تبدلت لي ألفا
titleEn: You Changed When You Exchanged My Thousand for Another
Harun, Best of Those We Hope In
Imminent Departure and Blazing Longing
You Know My Tears and My Longing
That Passion and That Fire Have Died
Modesty Silences Me Before You
By God, If You Felt What I Feel
My Father Be Ransom for Your Glance

titleAr: لهف نفسي علي لا بك عليكا
titleEn: I Grieve for Myself, Not for You

titleAr: إن حزني علي لا بل عليكا
titleEn: My Sorrow Is for You, Not for Me

titleAr: نم فإن لم أنم كراي كراكا
titleEn: Sleep, for If I Cannot Sleep My Sleep Is Yours

titleAr: يا أبا جعفر اقر لك الحس
titleEn: O Abu Jafar, Beauty Bows to You
My Comfort Is in Weeping Until I See You
I Am Stripped of Love Unless I Punish My Eyes
A King Who Oppresses When He Rules
Longing Has Kept Me Company Since You Left
The Envious Found Something to Say About Us
A Gazelle Whose Rose Dwells in His Cheek
How Long Drags My Endless Night

titleAr: شد ما استنزلتك من ربعك الأظ
titleEn: How Deeply the Ruins Drew You Down
A Singular Beauty, Offspring of Light
My Thought Summoned Him in Dreams
Cheeks Like Apples, Lips Like Wine
Love Is Unjust and You Are More So
Your Suspicion About What I Conceal Is Wise
The Beloved Visitor Has Come
Namesake of the Prophet
Sleep Is Forbidden to My Eye
O Gazelle With Rose-Petal Cheeks
Your Love Dwells Within My Sides
Time Is a Day of Joy, a Day of Blame
His Locks Are Alif and Lam
Do Not Shun, For Shunning Is Grave
Sheathe the Sword of Your Glance
A Fair Abode Appeared and He Wandered Mad
You Are Absolved, So Increase My Illness

titleAr: إذا راح مشهور المحاسن أو غدا
titleEn: When the Renowned Beauty Passes By
Distance Began As the Sin of Drawing Near
If Only You Could See Him, Abu al-Hasan
O Sleepless Eyelids I Have Deprived

titleAr: ومحتكم في الخمص طورا وفي البدن
titleEn: Ruling Between Slenderness and Fullness
By My Life, Some Eyes Found Rest Near You
Beauty Is But a Part of Your Beautiful Face
I Ransom Muhammad From All Harm
Be Gentle With Him If You Are His Master
She Gave, Then Lent, Then Took It Back

titleAr: منحتك ودا كان طفلا فقد نشا
titleEn: I Gave You Love That Was a Child Now Grown

titleAr: أشهى إلي من الدنيا وما فيها
titleEn: More Desired Than All the World
O You Who Shows No Mercy to Your Lovers
He Carries My Life in His Hands
I Revealed a Longing I Had Hidden

titleAr: لو كنت عندي أمس وهو معانقي
titleEn: Had You Been with Me Yesterday
His Beauty Surpassed All Description
My Opinion of Him Is Good Despite His Wrong
Trials of Time and the Poet's Perseverance
Gloom and Weeping Were Our Mounts
Days Sought Him as He Sought Them
When I Saw the Matter Was Serious
She Appeared as the Rope of Separation Tightened

titleAr: ألا صنع البين الذي هو صانع
titleEn: Separation Did What Separation Does
Tomorrow You Shall Die and Be Buried

titleAr: أرى ألفات قد خططن على راسي
titleEn: I See White Lines Drawn Upon My Head

titleAr: ألم يأن تركي لا علي ولا ليا
titleEn: Is It Not Time I Left Frivolity Behind
Separation Fed Me Colocynth's Bitter Brew
A Visitor Who Stirred a Phantom
I Am Jealous of My Own Kiss
Seeking My Death Through His Rejection
Handsome Yet Unjust to His Lover
How My Heart Was Humbled
O Sickness of the Eyelid from My Beloved
My Eye Translates What My Tongue Cannot Say

titleAr: كيف بعدي لا ذقتم البين أنتم
titleEn: How Have You Fared Since My Departure

titleAr: سلام على من لا يرد سلامي
titleEn: Greetings to One Who Does Not Return Them
Given the Finest Breath of Beauty
On Honor, Shame, and Character
A Scathing Invective Against a Rival
I Was Told Utba Is the Poet of the Rabble
Tell Abdun Where Has His Shame Gone
Utba the Most Cowardly of All Creation
My Poetry Pursues You Wherever You Flee

titleAr: من بنو عامر من ابن الحباب
titleEn: Who Are the Sons of Amir?
By Him Who Covered the Blessed with Disgrace
Honour Overflowed and Lineage Dried Up
Fire, Disgrace, and Ruin
O Yusuf, You Have Brought a Wonder
I Have Exhausted My Trials Among People

titleAr: إمرأة مقران ماتت بعد ما شابا
titleEn: Miqran's Wife Died After He Aged
Companions, Halt — I Have Had Enough of You
Your First Justice in My View
O Abd Allah, Leave Off Your Regrets
O Wife of the Wretch Miqran
Hold On — Brace for My Fury's Impact
A Mind That Profanes Idleness's Sanctuary
What Opinion or Sound Mind

titleAr: يا ابن تلك التي بحران لما
titleEn: O Son of That Woman in Harran

titleAr: قلبت أمري في بدء وفي عقب
titleEn: I Turned My Affairs in Beginning and End
I Was Told Utba Howls to Provoke Me
Now That the Watering Trough Is Exposed
Ayyash, O Master of Miserliness
Ayyash, Strenuous Effort Has Come to You
I Came to Yahya, Once My Friend
Do You String Together False and Senseless Words?
You Are Nothing but the Circulating Proverb
She Departed and Tears Replaced Pearls

titleAr: أبو تماملا تعجلن عليك بعد نهار
titleEn: Abu Tammam, Do Not Hurry — Tomorrow Comes
I Am Patient Despite What Has Befallen Me
May Your Faded Ruins Not Be Watered
O Noblest of Men in Fathers and Pride
Abdun's Land Is Well-Watered

titleAr: مضى ما كان قبل من الدعاره
titleEn: Gone Is the Former Debauchery
O Slave, You Have Become a Tale
O Gazelle, Speak to the Gazelle-Eyed
Cold and Miserable, You Are Excused

titleAr: نعم الفتى ابن الأعمش الغث الذفر
titleEn: What a Fine Youth, Son of Al-A'mash
I Knew When You Plucked You Would Be Arrogant
O Maqran, Son of Foreign Women
O Abd Allah, Rise and Sit With My Estrangement
I Bowed My Head Among My Companions
Maqran, O Branching-Headed One
The Heart Has Sobered After Its Trance
You Changed From Familiarity to Estrangement
By God, the Afflicted Son of Al-A'mash
Uthman, Do Not Busy Yourself With Muhammad's Mention
O One Whom God Has Turned Away
O Amr, Tell the Rising Moon
O Utba, If the Nights Grow Long
I Shall Satirize the Lowly Maqran
She Extended Slender Fingers Toward Me
Was He Not the Basil of the Describer
The Abode Speaks Yet Does Not Speak
Against Me Advances Utba the Clinging Wretch
Had I Not Been Fed Full of Foolishness
O Crescent Moon Now Overcome by Darkness
Leave the Wretched Son of Al-A'mash to Weep
Woe to You, Submit to the One Creator

titleAr: ماذا بدا لك إذ نقضت هواكا
titleEn: What Appeared to You When You Broke Your Love
Raging in Turbulence, Dissolute

titleAr: رغم أنفي من أن ترى مهتوكا
titleEn: My Nose Is Rubbed in the Dust Seeing You Exposed
Cut My Ropes, For I Am Fed Up With You
O Muwis, How Did You Find My Snares Laid
As If I Never Confided My Secret
I Heard Abdullah Has Begun to Wail
Your Love of Older Men Is Telling

titleAr: هل الله لو أشركت كان معذبي
titleEn: Would God Punish Me More Than This

titleAr: ستعلم يا عياش إن كنت تعلم
titleEn: You Will Know, O 'Ayyash
He Swore Truly by His Loaf of Bread
The Zanj and Romans Surpass You
Do You Know What Lightning Flash You Watch
Now You Have Let the Wolves Among the Sheep
Many a Coarse-Natured Man Excels
See How the Two New Ones Wear Us Away
The Days Have Exposed You, O Man
A Racing Horse of Streaming Swift Strides
Ahmad Has Gone and Left Me Sorrowful
Know the Mother of Ibn al-A'mash
Which Face Will You Show Me in Misr
Baghdad's Mourner Has Taken His Station

titleAr: لا ترث لابن الأعمش الكشخان من
titleEn: Do Not Pity Ibn al-A'mash the Cuckold
By Which Stars of Your Face Is Light Found
O Ornament of the World and Unifier of Its Ranks

titleAr: أبا دلف لم يبق طالب حاجة
titleEn: O Abu Dalaf, No Petitioner Remains but Me
Tell the Prince and Find Speech's Battleground

titleAr: صبرا على المطل مالم يتله الكذب
titleEn: Patience Under Delay When Lies Follow Not
None Knows but the Subtle, the Aware
She Turned Away the Flame of My Besotted Heart
They Laughed at Youth Departing in Regret
Your Pilgrimage Accepted and Your Sin Forgiven
O Abu Ali, Time's Turning Is Instructive

titleAr: ذل السؤال شجى في الحلق معترض
titleEn: The Humiliation of Asking Lodges in the Throat
A Brother Whom Time's Mixing Has Dictated To
White Hair Woven as a Veil
The Eye of the Yearning Youth Spoke
A Brother Whose Company Turned Bitter

titleAr: أجميل ما لك لا تجيب أخاكا
titleEn: O Jamil, Why Do You Not Answer?
I Witness: You Wore Abu Said's Generosity

titleAr: قد عرفناه دلائل المنع أو ما
titleEn: We Have Known the Signs of Withholding
Strange That Your Face Turns Away
I Am Ashamed That My Certainty Should Show Doubt
Know This, O Unteachable Man

titleAr: لا يحمد السجل حتى يحكم الوذم
titleEn: The Bucket Is Not Praised Until the Rope Is Sound
Muhammad ibn Said, Lend Me Your Ear
My Tribe and Ubaydallah's Are Reunited

titleAr: أبا القاسم أسلم في وفود من القسم
titleEn: Abu al-Qasim, May You Live Among Oaths of Safety
Were It Not for Old Bonds and Sanctity
Your Messenger on the Day of Battle Is the Lance

titleAr: ما ابيض وجه المرء في طلب العلى
titleEn: No Man's Face Whitens in Seeking Glory
A Blamer Whom I Blamed for His Blaming
Is There Among You a Young Man to Tell Me of Myself?
Did the Nobles of Maad and Madhij Ever Gather?
If Others Changed Under Wealth's Influence

titleAr: إذا ما شبت حسن الدين منك بصالح الأدب
titleEn: When Religion Is Perfected by Noble Conduct
We Seek What Has Departed — Farewell
Now I know resurrection is true
The raven, herald of tidings, claimed
O Sa'id, the matter concerning you is strange
Will your phantom never cease traveling to Zaynab
Did he visit after excessive estrangement
How many a sand-dune curves across a sand-dune
O Abu Hasan, fine consolation at calamity
How dear to me is the phantom of Su'da coming
Do you think my sorrows have not stirred
In folly her blame and insistence persisted

titleAr: دع الأمر لا تطلبه من نحو وجهه
titleEn: Leave the matter — do not seek it from its face

titleAr: كنت إلى وصل سعدى جد محتاج ،
titleEn: I was truly in need of Su'da's union
Yours among us are the easy generous manners

titleAr: أطاع عاذله ، في الحب ، إذ نصحا ،
titleEn: He obeyed his blamer in love when he counseled him
Is there in the first flow of streaming tears
He spent the night as my companion until morning

titleAr: وما خفت جدي في الصديق يسوءه ،
titleEn: I never feared my earnestness would harm a friend
My broad spaces have become those of Marw al-Shahijan

titleAr: العيش في ليل داريا ، إذا بردا ،
titleEn: True living is in the nights of Daraya when they cool
One who broke his promise

titleAr: أجرني من الواشي الذي جار واعتدى ،
titleEn: Protect me from the slanderer who wronged and transgressed
The only real folly is that Rashid should be reformed
Peace upon you, O full radiant moon
O appointment with her that I awaited

titleAr: سرى من خيال المالكية ما سرى ،
titleEn: What traveled from the phantom of the Malikiyya traveled
Praise be to God for what I see
I find whenever I seek longing I am capable of it
O you who takes back his little gift
How many a night in longing for you I spent sleepless
I am rid of al-Nughayl — how I despise him
I Protected My Soul from Defilement
Youth's Garment and Old Age's Acceptance
An Apology to Abu Ja'far
My Only Condition Is Fairness
Your Counsel O Abu Nahshal

titleAr: ترى الليل يقضي عقبة من هزيعه
titleEn: Does the Night Still Have Hours Remaining
He Burdened Me Beyond My Capacity
We Commend the Departing Traveler to God
The Brotherhood of Necessity Has Departed
Al-Kumaiti the Elder Abandoned Us
A Master Who Imposed Humiliation on Me
The Phantom of Mawiyya Visits Me
Greetings to the Summer and Spring Abodes
Hadramaut and Its Distant Tribes
A Swaying Beauty Far in Affection
By the One Who Gave You Virtue and Power
I Said to the Blamer at the Well: Wake Up

titleAr: أن رق لي قلبك مما ألاق
titleEn: If Your Heart Softened for My Suffering
Your Hands Drew Near with Noble Deeds
Will You Hear the One Who Calls to You
We Offer Ourselves for You: Time Is Relentless

titleAr: لو يكون الحباء حسب الذي أنت
titleEn: Were Gifts Commensurate to Your Worth
May My Father Be Your Ransom
Tell the Cloud Driven by the North Wind
Were You Not to Rebuke Me, I Would Say
People Warn Each Other Away from Me

titleAr: أبى الليل ، إلا أن يعود بطوله
titleEn: The Night Insists on Returning with Its Length
After Mubashshir and Abu Ubayd Are Gone
I Saw al-Bahbahani Burdened by Deprivation
Do the Ruins of Salma at Kadhima Still Stand
Tell the South Wind: Carry My Message
Banu Mukhlid, Restrain Your Generosity

titleAr: إذا شئت فاندبني إلى الراح ، وانعني
titleEn: Call Me to the Wine Cup When You Will
Rain Blessed al-Dhafafi's Land
We Have Lost Loyalty Like a Dear Friend
A Dream-Vision Visits Me in Sleep

titleAr: أعن جوار أبي إسحاق تطمع أن
titleEn: Do You Seek to Move Me from Abu Ishaq?
Take Up Longing or Bear Its Weight

titleAr: ملنا ، أم نبا بنا ، أم جفانا ،
titleEn: Did We Tire, or Did He Abandon Us?

titleAr: ليت الخليط الذي قد بان لم يبن
titleEn: Would That the Departed Companions Had Not Left
Sleeplessness for My Eye's Delight
Tell Abu Jafar: His Hand Reaches Far
The Ignoble One Satirized Me
A Young Man's Worth Is Not in Show
The Story of the Mound: Hear Its Wonders
God Bless You, O Jalalata
Time Is Bad and People Are Worse

titleAr: لنا أبدا يث نعانيه في أروى ،
titleEn: We Endure Eternal Grief for Arwa
The First Rains Came at Dawn

titleAr: يا عين ما لك لا تبكين تسكابا ؟
titleEn: O eye, why do you not weep
A vast wilderness like worn garments

titleAr: يا ابن الشريد ، على تنائي بيننا ،
titleEn: O son of al-Sharid, despite the distance between us
I lay awake while my companions slept

titleAr: ما بال عينيك منها دمعها سرب
titleEn: What ails your eyes that their tears flow

titleAr: يا عين جودي بدمع منك مسكوب
titleEn: O eye, pour forth your tears in streams

titleAr: تقول نساء : شبت من غير كبرة ،
titleEn: Women say I have aged without growing old
O eyes, weep for Sakhr in full measure

titleAr: لهفي على صخر فاني ارى له
titleEn: My grief for Sakhr — I see his generous gifts

titleAr: ألا يا عين فانهمري ، وقلت ألا يا عين فانهمري ، وقلت
titleEn: O eye, pour down — I said, O eye pour
O eye, be generous with tears

titleAr: ذري عنك أقوال الضلال ، كفى بنا
titleEn: Abandon the words of the misguided, we suffice
Do not think I have found any rest

titleAr: جرى لي طير في حمام حذرته
titleEn: An omen bird flew among doves, I warned you
O my two eyes, be generous and do not freeze
My eye wept and sleeplessness returned

titleAr: لا شيء يبقى غير وجه مليكنا
titleEn: Nothing endures save the face of our King
I weep for Sakhr when a ringed dove moans
O eye, be generous with tears, for your springs have dried

titleAr: أهاج لك الدموع على ابن عمر و
titleEn: What has stirred your tears over Ibn Amr
O my two eyes, pour generously your tears
The earth has grown narrow for me, its sacred bounds broken

titleAr: يا ابن الشريد وخير قيس كلها
titleEn: O son of al-Sharid, best of all Qays
I weep for my father Amr with a flowing eye
O eye, pour down with treachery's grief
Is there a mote in your eye or is it smarting
O my two eyes, will you not weep for Sakhr

titleAr: إن كنت عن وجدك لم تقصري
titleEn: If you have not fallen short in your grief
I remembered my brother after the carefree had slept
The death-news arrived at Safina at dawn
O sons of Sulaym, if you meet Faqas

titleAr: يا عين فيضي بدمع منك مغزار
titleEn: O eye, pour forth abundant tears

titleAr: عين فابكي لي على صخر إذا
titleEn: O eye, weep for me over Sakhr

titleAr: كأن ابن عمر و لم يصبح لغارة
titleEn: As though ibn Amr never rode to raid
O eye, pour forth your tears
Grief and sleeplessness visited me
My eyes, shed tears unstintingly

titleAr: يا عين جودي بدمع غير منزور
titleEn: O eye, weep without restraint
O eye, pour forth abundant tears
O Sakhr, who will face fate's trials
You called on Amir but cast him aside
We were like night stars with a moon among them
We were like two branches of a tall tree

titleAr: يا عين جودي بدمع منك مدرار
titleEn: O eye, pour forth streaming tears

titleAr: الا ابكي على صخر وصخر ثمالنا
titleEn: Weep for Sakhr, our mainstay in battle
Time has gnawed at me with biting and cutting
O sons of Sulaym, weep for your horseman
Memory keeps me sleepless at evening
O eye, weep for a horseman

titleAr: إن الزمان وما يفنى له عجب
titleEn: Time and its ceaseless consumption
As for the nights when I was a young girl
O eye, help me in my grief

titleAr: لقد صوت الناعي بفقد اخي الندى
titleEn: The announcer cried out the loss of my generous brother
Why does your eye not sleep
The long night refuses to let me sleep

titleAr: يا أم عمر و ألا تبكين معولة
titleEn: O mother of Amr, will you not wail
I remembered Sakhr when a dove sang
I swore I will not cease offering poems

titleAr: يا عين بكي بدمع غير إنزاف
titleEn: O eye, weep with inexhaustible tears

titleAr: ما لذا الموت لا يزال مخيفا
titleEn: Why does death remain forever fearsome

titleAr: يا لهف نفسي على صخر وقد لهفت
titleEn: My soul aches for Sakhr
My eye is sore from weeping
Pour your tears or find patience

titleAr: يا عين جودي بدمع منك مهراق
titleEn: O eye, pour out your tears
Events of time make your eye flow
O my two eyes, weep and wail
My eye wept and its wailing was right

titleAr: آلا ليت أمي لم تلدني سوية
titleEn: Would my mother had never borne me
O eye, be generous with flowing tears
The canopy of Abu Hassan has fallen
I weep for the hero Sakhr
O my eye, overflow and be not stingy

titleAr: ألا يا صخر إن أبكيت عيني
titleEn: O Sakhr, you made me weep
What ails your eye, what is wrong with it
When I saw the full moon eclipse
May rain water the grave beyond Ghamra

titleAr: كل امرىء باثا في الدهر مرجوم
titleEn: Every man who tarries in time is struck
My soul is ransom for the Jashmite horseman

titleAr: من لامني في حب كوز وذكره
titleEn: Whoever blames me for loving Kūz
By my life — and my life is no small thing
Convey to Sulaym and her satiated kin
O eye, pour out your tears abundantly

titleAr: أمن ذكر صخر دمع عينه يسجم
titleEn: Is it remembrance of Sakhr that streams

titleAr: يا عين بكي على صخر لأشجان
titleEn: O eye, weep for Sakhr in grief

titleAr: يا لهف نفسي على صخر وقد فزعت
titleEn: My soul aches for Sakhr when war alarmed
My eye wept and its mote returned
Who has seen my two brothers for me
O rooster calling at dawn

titleAr: ارى الدهر افنى معشري وبني أبي
titleEn: I see time has destroyed my kin and my father's sons

titleAr: الا لا ارى في الناس مثل معاويه
titleEn: None among the people equals Muawiya
Daughter of Sakhr, She Who Weeps
It Profits Not in My Creed
All of Life Is Toil

titleAr: وَلَمَّا رَأَيْتُ الجَهْلَ فِي النَّاسِ فَاشِيًا
titleEn: When I Saw Ignorance Spreading Among People
Let No Clouds Rain upon Me
This One Whom His Mother Taught
The Mu'allaqa of Antara
Love for Abla
Boast of Valor
Proclaim My Glory
Longing for Abla

titleAr: كان مغرما بابنة عمه عبلة فقل أن تخلو له قصيدة من ذكرها. اجتمع في شبابه بامرئ القيس الشاعر،
titleEn: He Was Enamored with His Cousin Abla

titleAr: إن كان يسعدني الزمان فإنني في همتي لصروفه
titleEn: If Fate Should Aid Me in My Resolve

titleAr: فلئن بقيت لأصنعن عجائبا ولأبكمنن بلاغة الفصحاءما
titleEn: If I Live I Shall Perform Wonders

titleAr: ولكن تبعد الفحشاء عني كبعد الأرض عن جو السماءلئن
titleEn: But Vulgarity Stays Far from Me

titleAr: يا طامعا في هلاكي عد بلا طمع ولا ترد كأس حتف أنت
titleEn: O You Who Crave My Ruin, Turn Back

titleAr: والنقع يوم طراد الخيل يشهد لي والضرب والطعن والأقلام والكتبلا
titleEn: The Battle Dust Bears Witness for Me

titleAr: قتلنا منهم مائتين حرا وألفا في الشعاب وفي الهضابألا
titleEn: We Slew Two Hundred of Them Free Men

titleAr: ولا تسقني كأس المدام فإنها يضل بها عقل الشجاع ويذهبسلا
titleEn: Do Not Pour Me Wine for It Leads Brave Men Astray

titleAr: وغادرت نضلة في معرك يجر الأسنة
titleEn: I Left Nudla in a Battle Dragging Spears

titleAr: كتائب تزجى فوق كل كتيبة لواء كظل الطائر المتقلبكأن
titleEn: Battalions Driving Above Each Battalion a Banner

titleAr: وأنا امرؤ إن يأخذوني عنوة أقرن إلى شرالركاب وأجنبلا
titleEn: I Am a Man Who If Taken by Force

titleAr: ودعوني أجر ذيل فخار عندما تخجل الجبان
titleEn: Let Me Trail the Robe of Glory

titleAr: فمن أجاب نجا مما يحاذره ومن أبى طعم الحرب والحربدعني
titleEn: Whoever Answers Is Saved from What He Fears

titleAr: مقامك في جو السماء مكانه وباعي قصير عن نوال الكواكبأعاتب
titleEn: Your Station Is in the Vault of Heaven

titleAr: وأترك كل نائحة تنادي عليهم بالتفرق والشتاتإذا
titleEn: I Leave Every Wailing Woman Calling upon Them

titleAr: ولي بيت علا فلك الثريا تخر لعظم هيبته
titleEn: I Have a House That Towers above the Pleiades

titleAr: ألا إنها خير القصائد كلها يفصل منها كل ثوب
titleEn: Truly This Is the Best of All Poems

titleAr: كسوت الجعد جعد بني أبان سلاحي بعد عري
titleEn: I Clothed the Curly-Haired One of Banu Aban with My Armor

titleAr: يجررن هاما فلقتها رماحنا تزيل منهن اللحى والمسايحطربت
titleEn: They Drag Skulls Our Spears Have Split

titleAr: فقد أمكنت منك الأسنة عانيا فلم تجز إذ تسعى قتيلا
titleEn: The Spears Have Got You Captive

titleAr: قصائد من قيل امرىء يحتذيكم بني العشراء فارتدوا
titleEn: Poems of a Man Whose Example You Should Follow

titleAr: كأن رماحهم أشطان بئر لها في كل مدلجة خدودتركت
titleEn: Their Spears Like Well-Ropes in Every Dark Path

titleAr: إذا قيل من للمعضلات أجابه عظام اللهى منا طوال
titleEn: When Asked Who Answers Hardships

titleAr: أقمت الحق في الهندي رغما وأظهرت الضلال من
titleEn: I Established Right with the Indian Sword Forcibly

titleAr: وتسهر لي أعين الحاسدين وترقد أعين أهل
titleEn: The Eyes of the Envious Stay Sleepless for Me

titleAr: وأما القائلون قتيل طعن فذلك مصرع البطل
titleEn: And Those Who Say He Was Slain by a Thrust

titleAr: وعدنا بالنهاب وبالسرايا وبالأسرى تكبل بالصفادصحا
titleEn: We Returned with Plunder and Raiding Parties

titleAr: إذا عادت بنو الأعجام تهوي وقد ولت ونكست البنوداألا
titleEn: When the Foreigners Return Falling in Defeat

titleAr: ولولا صارمي وسنان رمحي لما رفعت بنو عبس
titleEn: Were It Not for My Sword and My Spear-Tip

titleAr: بهاليل مثل الأسد في كل موطن كأن دم الأعداء في فمهم
titleEn: Noble Men Like Lions in Every Station
Rise to Take Vengeance
Tell Husn of My Enmity
Time Struck Me Like a Traitor

titleAr: ألا قاتل الله الهوى كم بسيفه
titleEn: Curse the Sword of Love
I Shall Soon Break Their Captivity
I Humbled the Enviers by My Might
If My Eyes See the Riding Camels

titleAr: حوى كل حسن في الكواعب شخصها
titleEn: Her Form Gathers All Beauty

titleAr: لئن تشمت الأعداء يا بنت مالك
titleEn: Though Enemies Gloat, Daughter of Malik
I Met Their Cavalry with My Own
You Had No Right to Insult Us
Every Steed Swimming in the Dust

titleAr: إن عشت فهي التي ما عشت مالكتي
titleEn: If I Live She Owns Me, If I Die
How Can I Bear Patience from My Beloved
I Shall Seek Him in Your Land
I Left Ruins Where a Company Dwelt
One Like Me Does Not Flinch
I Drove My Sword Through Souls
When Heroes Flee My Might
She Bore It with Knowing Patience
Another I Dragged Along My Spear
Gleaming Battalions Above All Others

titleAr: وإن يعيبوا سوادا قد كسيت به
titleEn: If They Fault My Darkness

titleAr: قد أطعن الطعنة النجلاء عن عرض
titleEn: I Deliver the Wide Devastating Thrust

titleAr: عمرو بن أسود فا زباء قاربة
titleEn: Amr ibn Aswad by the Water of Kullab
You Will Learn When Our Cavalry Meets

titleAr: ما سابق الناس يوم الفضل مكرمة
titleEn: None Has Outrun Me to Honor
Perhaps He Will Grant My Uncle's Demand
Give Them Your Final Counsel
How Many Lords I Left Fallen
Were It Not For God's Power

titleAr: ريح الحجاز بحق من أنشاك ردي السلام
titleEn: Wind of Hijaz, Carry My Greeting

titleAr: وما كنت لولا حب عبلة حائلا
titleEn: Without Abla's Love I Would Not Have Stayed
Long Have I Lingered at the Ruins
Ubaylahs Wonder at the Warrior
Our Spears Suffice Our Household's Need
Tears That Stream Upon the Cheeks
They Relieved My Anguish and Healed My Ailment
When the East Wind Blows at Dusk
I Fight Every Tyrant Yet Separation Kills Me
Perhaps the Days Will Bring Relief Soon
The Generous Feeders in Years of Drought

titleAr: لا يشرب الخمر إلا من له ذمم
titleEn: Only the Honorable May Drink Wine
Life with Humiliation Is Hell
A Heart the Blamer Cannot Sway
Come Fight Me, O Misfortunes of Nights

titleAr: سلي يا عبل عمرا عن فعالي
titleEn: Ask Amr About My Deeds, O Abla
He Desires the One Who Torments Him

titleAr: دع ما مضى لك في الزمان الأول
titleEn: Leave What Has Passed in Former Times
The Punishment of Estrangement Brought Union

titleAr: يا صاحبي لا تبك ربعا قد خلا
titleEn: Friend, Do Not Weep at the Empty Campsite
Have the Poets Left Any Theme Untouched
With Every Keen Blade That Pierces Through
Ruqash Comes Only for Brief Visits
Abla Lingers in Her Veil While I Fight
I Wept at Bitter Separation Though Steadfast
That Is Abla's Fire, O My Companion
One Day in Honor's Shade Surpasses a Thousand

titleAr: أحب بني عبس ولو هدروا دمي
titleEn: I Love the Banu Abs Even If They Shed My Blood
A Heart That Wine Cannot Console
Nothing Startled Me but Separation and Its Cruelty
He Answers the Conscience's Subtle Signals
The Women Cry Out in Fear of Captivity
Halt, My Two Friends at Dawn, and Greet

titleAr: عليك أيا عبيلة كل يوم سلام
titleEn: Upon You, O Ubaylah, Each Day a Peace

titleAr: أنا في الحرب العوان غير مجهول المكان
titleEn: In the Raging War I Am Not Unknown

titleAr: أحبك يا ظلوم فأنت عندي مكان الروح
titleEn: I Love You, O Unjust One, You Are to Me Like the Soul
O King Whose Palms Stand in Place of Rain
When My Adversary Demands His Debt

titleAr: يا طائر البان قد هيجت أشجاني
titleEn: O Bird of the Ban Tree, You Have Stirred My Sorrows
For Whom Are These Ruins at al-Raqmatain That Grieve Me
O Dwelling, Where Have the Inhabitants Departed
Ask, O Abla of the Two Mountains, About Us

titleAr: ألا يا غراب البين في الطيران
titleEn: O Raven of Parting in Its Flight
He Was in Battle Defending Its Sacred Trust

titleAr: أرى لي كل يوم مع زماني عتابا
titleEn: I See Myself Each Day Reproached by My Age
Longing Stirred Me and the Yemeni Lightning Moved Me
I Recalled My Longing After a Long Time

titleAr: يا عبل أين من المنية مهربي
titleEn: O Abla, Where Is My Escape from Death
I Am Not Abandoning You, But
Halt at the Dwellings and Call Out to Their Expanse
Ask Juhayna About Us — How Did She Spend the Night
With My Sword I Established the Market of Death
O Dwelling of Abla at al-Tawa
May God Requite the Ancient Crumbling Ruins
Record! I Am an Arab

titleAr: عَلَى هَذِهِ الأَرْضِ مَا يَسْتَحِقُّ الحَيَاةَ
titleEn: On This Earth, There Is What Deserves Life
I Long for My Mother's Bread
To My Mother
In Jerusalem
In Praise of the Prophet ﷺ
Satire Against Quraysh at Badr
In Defense of Islam
Elegy for the Prophet ﷺ
The Day of the Conquest
The Traces of Dhat al-Asabi' Are Effaced

titleAr: وأحسن منك لم تر قط عيني
titleEn: More Beautiful Than You No Eye Has Seen
Does a Faded Trace of the Dwelling Answer?
I Recognized Zaynab's Dwelling at the Sand-Dune
My Night at Juman Grew Long

titleAr: إن تمس دار ابن أروى منه خالية
titleEn: If the House of Ibn Arwa Lies Empty
What Did You Resent of Worn Garments?
When the Adal Were Driven to Us
God's Blessings on Those Who Fell in Succession
I Swore an Oath That Was No Lie
She Said to Him One Day, Speaking to Him
The Place of Adhib Has Faded After Us
Then By God We Shall Hurl War Upon Them
Fayruz Bereaved Us — May He Have No Worth
We Were Absent from the Plain of Mecca

titleAr: يا حار ، قد عولت ، غير معول ،
titleEn: O Har, You Relied on What Cannot Be Relied Upon
When Quraysh Traces Its Lineage, They Reject You

titleAr: يا حار قد كنت لولا ما رميت به ،
titleEn: O Har, You Would Have Been — Were It Not for What You Did

titleAr: يا عين جودي بدمع منك منسكب ،
titleEn: O Eye, Pour Forth Your Flowing Tears
Baseness Built a House Upon Madhhij
Who Will Inform Safwan That His Old Woman
By God, Hudhayl Does Not Know
Muzayna — No Orator Is Seen Among Them
When Quraysh Is Counted or Registered
Hudhayl Asked the Messenger of God an Obscenity

titleAr: يا حار إن كنت أمرأ متوسعا
titleEn: O Har, If You Are a Man of Means
Your Father Is Your Father, and You Are His Son
You Boasted of the Banner — What Wretched Boasting
Ask Quraysh and Their Allies

titleAr: ذكرت القروم الصيد من آل هاشم ،
titleEn: I Recalled the Noble Chiefs of the Hashim Clan
By Your Life, Umayya Did Not Bequeath
Convey to Usayd a Message from Me
When Umm Amr Saw Me She Turned Away
Who Will Carry the Rhymes After Hassan and His Son?
Hakim Was Saved at Badr by His Horse's Gallop
Tall of Sword-Belt, Lofty of Tent-Pole
Tell Rabi'a and His Cousin Nawfal

titleAr: يا دوس ، إن أبا أزيهر أصبحت
titleEn: O Daws, Abu Uzayhir's Echoes Are Gone
The Banu Asad Have Failed
Al-Awwam Only Insulted Me Because
Radiant, Upon Him the Seal of Prophethood
Clad in Rings of Iron They Are Led
By God My Lord, We Shall Not Forsake the Noble One

titleAr: لقد خاب قوم غاب عنهم نبيهم ،
titleEn: Truly Lost Are the People from Whom Their Prophet Departed

titleAr: ألم تر أن الغدر واللؤم والخنا
titleEn: Do You Not See That Treachery and Baseness
In Tayba There Is a Trace of the Messenger

titleAr: ما بال عينك لا تنام كأنما
titleEn: What Ails Your Eye That It Cannot Sleep?

titleAr: آليت ما في جميع الناس مجتهدا ،
titleEn: I Swear with the Utmost Sincerity

titleAr: متى يبد في الداجي البهيم جبينه
titleEn: Whenever His Brow Appears in the Pitch-Dark Night

titleAr: ألا دفنتم رسول الله في سفط
titleEn: Would That You Had Buried the Messenger of God
Did You Abandon the Frontier and Come Instead

titleAr: ماذا أردتم من أخي الخير باركت
titleEn: What Did You Want from the Man of Goodness?

titleAr: أمسى الخلابيس قد عزوا وقد كثروا ،
titleEn: The Riffraff Have Grown Mighty and Multiplied
Is There One Who Will Convey to Rabi'a from Me?
Did the Sons of the Foundling Woman Rejoice?
Look, My Friend, in the Heart of Jilliq
Convey to All Who Listen of a Battle

titleAr: تروح من الحسناء أم أنت مغتدي ،
titleEn: Do You Depart from the Beauty or Do You Stay?

titleAr: لعمر أبيك الخير ، يا شعث ، ما نبا
titleEn: By Your Good Father's Life, O Sha'th

titleAr: ومن عاش منا عاش في عنجهية
titleEn: Whoever Among Us Lives, Lives in Proud Defiance

titleAr: لو كنت من هاشم ، أو من بني أسد ،
titleEn: If You Were from Hashim or Banu Asad
Has the Eye Not Known Its Sleeplessness?
If You Reform, You Are Still an Abidi
Mongrels When Their Lineage Is Traced
We Are Not Drinkers Beneath a Cloak's Shade
By God, I Do Not Know — Though I Ask
The Merciful Has Cursed a Host Led Astray
The Vile Son of Nabigha Claimed
I Asked All of Quraysh — Their Worst Are Banu Abid
If You Seek the Mightiest Chief
I Am the Son of Khalda and al-Agharr

titleAr: فمن يك منهم ذا خلاق ، فإنه
titleEn: Whoever Among Them Has Good Character

titleAr: لعمرك ما تنفك عن طلب الخنا
titleEn: By Your Life, They Never Cease Seeking Disgrace

titleAr: لقد كان قيس في اللئام مرددا ،
titleEn: Qays Was Tossed About Among the Base

titleAr: وما طلعت شمس النهار ولا بدت
titleEn: No Sun Has Risen Nor Appeared Upon You with Glory
Whose Is the Child at the Edge of the Valley?
Whose Are These Abandoned Infants?

titleAr: لقد علم الأقوام أن ابن هاشم
titleEn: The People Know That the Son of Hashim
May God Have Mercy on Nafi ibn Budayl

titleAr: غدا أهل حضني ذي المجاز بسحرة
titleEn: The People of the Slopes of Dhu al-Majaz Set Out at Dawn

titleAr: شق له من اسمه كي يجله ،
titleEn: He Derived for Him from His Name to Honor Him
Tell the Destitute That Goodness Has Left Them
You Were the Pupil of My Eye
Beware — I Have Grown Old and Trials Overcame Me
Al-Nadira, the Lady of the Curtained Chamber
A Harsh Night in Yathrib Revisited Me
I Was Told That Abu Mundhir
O Eye, Pour Forth Your Scanty Tears

titleAr: أوفت بنو عمرو بن عوف نذرها ،
titleEn: Banu Amr ibn Awf Fulfilled Their Vow

titleAr: وأفلت يوم الروع أوس بن خالد ،
titleEn: Aws ibn Khalid Escaped on the Day of Terror
She Asks about a Noble Champion

titleAr: ألا ليت شعري هل اتى أهل مكة
titleEn: Has the News Reached the People of Mecca
Weep for the Slain of Ma'una
The Son of Wudd Lies Fallen

titleAr: لست إلى عمرو ، ولا المرء منذر ،
titleEn: I Am Not Beholden to Amr Nor to Mundhir
God Curse the Dwelling of Kawtha
I Asked Quraysh and They Did Not Lie
Sorrows Have Increased and Tears Flow

titleAr: على حين أن قالتا لأيمن أمه :
titleEn: When His Mother Said to Ayman
Quraysh Was an Egg That Cracked Open

titleAr: إني لأعجب من قول غررت به ،
titleEn: I Marvel at a Speech That Deceived You
Sulaym Has Raged in Ignorant Folly
Amra Has Resolved to Sever, So Set Forth at Dawn

titleAr: قد أصبح القلب عنها كاد يصرفه
titleEn: The Heart Has Nearly Turned Away from Her

titleAr: رميت بها أهل المضيق ، فلم تكد
titleEn: I Aimed at the People of the Narrow Pass
Show Me Fortunes Like the Fortunes That Rose

titleAr: ما البكر إلا كالفصيل وقد ترى
titleEn: The Young Camel Is Like the Weaned Calf

titleAr: وأمانة المري ، حيث لقيته ، يا حار من يغدر بذمة جاره
titleEn: The Trustworthiness of the Murri

titleAr: ما ولدتكم قروم من بني أسد ،
titleEn: No Noble Stallions of Banu Asad Sired You
Uyayna Thought When He Raided Her

titleAr: حار بن كعب ألا الأحلام تزجركم
titleEn: O Harith ibn Ka'b, Do Your Minds Not Restrain You
By Your Life, in the Valley between Ma'rif
His Lightning Struck Busra
Salama Is a Doll in a Door's Panel

titleAr: يا ابني رفاعة ، ما بالي وبالكما ،
titleEn: O Two Sons of Rifa'a, What Is Between Me and You

titleAr: أبلغ معاوية بن حرب مألكا ، أبلغ معاوية بن حرب مألكا ،
titleEn: Convey a Message to Mu'awiya ibn Harb
A People Whose Hatred Is Crooked
Qurayza Has Met What Grieves Them
Quraysh Plastered the Basins of Glory
A Base People Whose Goodness God Has Curtailed
I Do Not Revile the Hammas Tribe
She Pointed and Her Habit Was Baseness
Your Vile Father Was a Disgrace

titleAr: لو كان ، في الدار ، قوم ذو محافظة ،
titleEn: Had There Been Guardians in the Abode

titleAr: يا آل بكر ألا تنهون جاهلكم ،
titleEn: O Banu Bakr Restrain Your Fool
Whose Abandoned House Stands at Buwat

titleAr: بني أسد ، ما بال آل خويلد
titleEn: O Banu Asad Why Does Khuwaylid's Clan
Spurious Words from Umayya Reached Me

titleAr: منعنا رسول الله ، إذ حل وسطنا ،
titleEn: We Protected God's Messenger Among Us
The Nobles of Fihr and Their Brothers
Lightning Kept Me Awake at Sla and Fari

titleAr: ألا يا لقوم هل لما حم دافع ؟
titleEn: Is There Any Repelling of What Fate Decrees
Lamis Has Departed with Severed Bonds
Do the Ruins of Umm al-Walid Stir You
Turn Away from Foul Words When You Hear Them
Hell's Guards Around Their Tents
Ask Banu al-Ash'ar When You Visit Them
I Remind Banu al-Najjar of My Father's Deeds
By God Ma'is Does Not Know Mecca's Terrain

titleAr: لقد أتى عن بني الجرباء قولهم ،
titleEn: The Words of Banu al-Jarba Have Reached Me
Time Has Come for a Famous Poem

titleAr: بني القين هلا إذ فخرتم بربعكم
titleEn: O Banu al-Qayn Why Do You Boast of Your Quarter

titleAr: وما سارق الدرعين ، إن كنت ذاكرا ،
titleEn: The Thief of Two Coats of Mail Is No Nobleman
How Excellent the Band That Met Them
Whose House and Effaced Traces Remain
We Cut the Ears of Ka'b and Amir

titleAr: لو أن اللؤم ينسب كان عبدا
titleEn: If Baseness Had a Lineage It Would Be a Slave
Did Banu Bakr Think the Prophet's Letter a Trifle
O Mal the Turbaned Chief May Be Led Astray
Umayya ibn Khalaf Satirized Hassan
Deliver to Banu Juhjaба and Their People
Why Does Your Eye Restrain Its Tears

titleAr: ألم ترنا أولاد عمرو بن عامر ،
titleEn: Do You Not See Us, Sons of Amr ibn Amir

titleAr: ما بال عينك لا ترقا مدامعها ،
titleEn: Why Does Your Eye Not Stop Its Flowing Tears
When God Greets a Folk Through Their Deeds
Poetry Is the Mind of a Man Displayed
We Camped by the Exhausted Well for Many Nights

titleAr: فإن تك عنا ، معشر الأسد ، سائلا ،
titleEn: If You Ask About Us, O Tribe of Asad
Let My Mother Be a Ransom for All of Awf
Who Will Deliver a Message to Hassan from Me
My Father's Faithfulness Runs Deep

titleAr: إذا تذكرت شجوا من أخي ثقة ،
titleEn: When You Remember Sorrow from a Trusted Brother
O Raven of Separation, You Heard — Now Speak
The Battle That Seized Ibn al-Zub'ari
Soft-Sandaled Men of Noble Virtue

titleAr: أسألت رسم الدار أم لم تسأل
titleEn: Did You Ask the Traces of the Dwelling, or Did You Not
Did the Traces of the Abodes Stir You in the Wilderness
Deliver My Message to Abu Makhzum

titleAr: يا حار ! في سنة من نوم أولكم ،
titleEn: O Harith! Are You in the Slumber of Your Ancestors
I Bear Witness That Muhammad Is God's Messenger
We Repelled the Wrong Done to Us Despite the Tribes
A Chaste, Dignified Woman Above All Suspicion
How Many Months and Changes Have the Abodes Known
We Were the Kings of the People Before Muhammad
Do You Recognize the Dwelling Whose Traces Have Faded
Qurayza Met What Destroyed It
They Aided Their Prophet and Strengthened His Arm
My Father Fears the Enemy's Heart
I Accepted the Judgment of al-Marqal Qays
A Weighty Verse That Rang Through the Night
I Wept at the Death of Ja'far
He Upheld the Prophet's Covenant and Guidance
Friends in Ease Are Many
I Knew You as a Chaste Believer
He Inherited Misguidance from His Father
When the Thaqafi Boasts Against You, Say:
Muzayna Came from Amq to Aid Them
Tell Ubayd That Boasting Is a Defect
Banu Asad's Numbers Deserve No Fear
His People Named Him Abu Hakam
Thaqif Has Always Been Base
On the Day of Badr We Met You with Reinforcement
Baseness Surpasses All of Thaqif
How Badly Khaybar Fought to Defend Its Wealth
You Are Not of the Noblest Company

titleAr: لك الخير غضي اللوم عني فإنني
titleEn: Spare Me Your Blame, for I Love the Finest Character
Are You Unmoved by the Ruins of Dwellings?
O Sons of Hamas, Is There None Noble Among You?

titleAr: إذا قال لم يترك مقالا لقائل ،
titleEn: When He Speaks He Leaves No Room for Others

titleAr: لا تعد من رجلا أحلك بغضه
titleEn: Do Not Count One Whose Hatred Has Cast You Here
A Maiden Enslaved Your Heart in a Dream
By Your Life, Your Kinship with Quraysh Is False
Would That Khubaib Had Not Been Betrayed
When You See Two Shepherds with Sheep
Did You Not Ask the New Campsite to Speak?
Those Are My People — If You Ask
Sorrows Prevented Sleep at Evening
What Stirred Hassan: Ruins and a Vision

titleAr: هل المجد إلا السودد العود والندى ،
titleEn: Is Glory Anything But Ancient Lordship and Generosity?
Weep — Your Eyes Have Wept and Then Rushed Forth

titleAr: ما بال عينك ، يا حسان ، لم تنم ،
titleEn: Why Do Your Eyes Not Sleep

titleAr: ألين ، إذا لان العشير ، فإن تكن
titleEn: I Am Gentle When Companions Are Gentle
Chosroes Struck Me with Misery
God Honored Us with His Prophet's Victory

titleAr: إن ابن جفنة من بقية معشر
titleEn: The Son of Jafna, Remnant of a Noble Kin
Whose Is This Faded Dwelling?

titleAr: أعين ، ألا أبكي سيد الناس ، واسفحي
titleEn: O Eyes, Weep for the Master of Men

titleAr: إني ، لعمر أبيك ، شر من أبي ،
titleEn: By Your Father's Life, I Am the Worse
Inform the Banu Amr of Their Brother
Suqa'b, Your Father's Ancestor, a Blacksmith

titleAr: باهى ابن صقعب ، إذ أثرى ، بكلبته ،
titleEn: Ibn Suqa'b Boasted of His Dog in Wealth
Banu al-Najjar Know My Loyalty
Claiming Descent from Qusayy Is Forbidden
I Asked Quraysh and They Knew Well
Quraysh Attained Glory But Banu al-Mughira Fell
By Abu Sumayya's Life, I Care Not

titleAr: ألم تر أن طلحة من قريش
titleEn: Do You Not See That Talha Is of Quraysh
When Aquila Is Mentioned with Disgrace
O Abu Lahab, Know That Muhammad Shall Prevail

titleAr: من سره الموت صرفا لا مزاج له ،
titleEn: Whoever Desires Pure Death Without Mixture
O Men, For Tears Stirred by Grief
The One Who Steals Sips of Wine Submissive
The One Holding His Aching Head from Drink
If You Ask, We Are a Noble Kin
The Bloom of Youth and Black Hair
We Used to Say When We Saw Such Beauty
Whose Dwelling Grew Desolate at Ma'an
Yathrib Knows We Are Her Balance

titleAr: إن سرك الغدر صرفا لا مزاج له ،
titleEn: If You Love Pure Treachery Without Mixture
Convey to Abu Qays a Message
O Rider, Deliver My Message
Convey From Me to Banu al-Dayyan
When the Youth Among Us Grows Up
You Led Kinana to Their Ruin
If Baseness Were Made Flesh
Convey to Hawazin High and Low

titleAr: ثوى في قريش ، بضع عشرة حجة ،
titleEn: He Dwelt Among Quraysh for Years
Our Father Malik Left His Counsel
We Drank upon the Remembrance of the Beloved (al-Khamriyya)
My Heart Tells Me You Are My Destroyer
Increase My Bewilderment in Excessive Love of You

titleAr: هَلْ نَارُ لَيْلَى بَدَتْ لَيْلًا بِذِي سَلَمِ
titleEn: Did the Fire of Layla Appear by Night at Dhi Salam?
O Driver of the Camel Litters, Folding the Desert
Distance Has Become a Substitute for Our Closeness (The Nuniyya)
I Remembered You at al-Zahra with Longing
O You Who Turn Away from Us
My Suspicion Does No Harm
Bid Patience Farewell, for a Lover Has Bid You Farewell
Let the Days Do What They Will

titleAr: إِذَا المَرْءُ لَا يَرْعَاكَ إِلَّا تَكَلُّفًا
titleEn: If a Person Only Tends to You Out of Obligation
We Blame Our Times Though the Fault Is Ours
I Have Tested the People of This World
Lions Die of Hunger in the Forests
Do You Mock Supplication?
People Have Said Much About Women
What Grief for the Youth
I Have Become Cast Aside Among the Ignorant
The Fire of My Soul Dimmed as My Temples Grayed
When a Vile Person Insults Me, I Rise Higher
The Fool Addresses Me With Every Foulness
Among the Afflictions Is to Love
Tell the Astrologer About Me
You Are Sufficient for Me
When You Are Confused Between Two Matters

titleAr: أرى الغر في الدنيا إذا كان فاضلا
titleEn: I See the Noble in This World, if Virtuous

titleAr: ما في المقام لذي عقل وذي أدب
titleEn: There Is No Rest for the Wise and Cultured
I Shall Journey Across the Length and Breadth of Lands
He Who Respects Men, They Will Respect Him

titleAr: لما عفوت ولم أحقد على أحد
titleEn: When I Forgave and Bore No Grudge

titleAr: يا لهف نفسي على مال أفرقه
titleEn: How I Wish I Had Wealth to Distribute
The Judges of the Age Have Gone Astray
Money Made People Speak After Their Silence
The Prophet's Family Are My Means

titleAr: اصبر على مر الجفا من معلم
titleEn: Endure the Bitterness of a Teacher's Harshness
I Love Among Brothers Every Agreeable One
What Will Your Guest Tell His Family?
Many a Calamity That Overwhelms a Man
Beautiful Patience -- How Near Is Relief!

titleAr: قالوا سكت وقد خوصمت قلت لهم
titleEn: They Said: You Were Silent When Quarreled With
Be Both a Jurist and a Sufi, Not Just One
The Trials of Time Are Many and Unending
They Said: You Have Become a Rafidi
If Only Dogs Were Our Neighbors

titleAr: تمنى رجال أن أموت وإن أمت
titleEn: Men Wished That I Would Die
When I Came to People Seeking from Them

titleAr: إني صحبت أناسا ما لهم عدد
titleEn: I Have Accompanied Countless People
He Who Exhausts His Camels Traveling to a Land

titleAr: عفا الله عن عبد أعان بدعوة
titleEn: May God Pardon the Servant Who Helped with a Prayer

titleAr: إن كنت تغدو في الننوب جليدا
titleEn: If You Go About Boldly in Sins
If I Have My Day's Provision When Morning Comes
Were It Not That Poetry Disgraces Scholars
I See Rest for the Truth When It Is Fulfilled
Man Wishes to Be Given His Desires

titleAr: يا من يعانق دنيا لا بقاء لها
titleEn: O You Who Embrace a World That Does Not Last
Rain Down Pearls, O Mountains of Sarandib

titleAr: الدهر يومان ذا أمن وذا خطر
titleEn: Time Is Two Days: One of Safety, One of Peril
I Found My Silence to Be a Profitable Trade
The Lame One Grew Proud and Danger Rose with Him
Accept the Excuses of Those Who Come Apologizing

titleAr: إذا ما كنت ذا فضل وعلم
titleEn: If You Are a Person of Virtue and Knowledge

titleAr: إذا لم أجد خلا تقيا فوحدتي
titleEn: If I Cannot Find a Pious Friend, Then My Solitude

titleAr: كن سائرا في ذا الزمان بسيره
titleEn: Walk in This Age with Its Own Gait
A Friend Who Does Not Help on a Day of Hardship
My Heart, O God, Is Illuminated by Your Mercy

titleAr: يا وعظ الناس عما أنت قاعله
titleEn: O You Who Preach to People Against What You Yourself Do
Extracting a Tooth and Being Imprisoned
Knowledge Is the Nursery of All Pride

titleAr: شهدت بأن الله لا رب غيره
titleEn: I Bear Witness That God Has No Lord Besides Him
I Complained to Waki' About My Poor Memory

titleAr: إذا لم تجودوا والأمور بكم تمضى
titleEn: If You Are Not Generous While Affairs Go Your Way
Advise Me in Private
If a Man Is Wise and Pious
Sufficient for Me Is the Knowledge That It Benefits
How Many an Oppressor I Was Spared From Fighting
You Disobey God While Claiming to Love Him

titleAr: سل المفتي المكي من آل هاشم
titleEn: Ask the Meccan Mufti from the House of Hashim
The Slave Is Free If He Is Content
He Adorned the Lands and All Upon Them
The Eagle Eats Carrion by Its Strength

titleAr: ارحل بنفسك من أرض تضام بها
titleEn: Depart from a Land Where You Are Oppressed
My Wakefulness to Refine Knowledge Is Sweeter to Me
If You Hear That a Fortunate Man Acquired
If a Man Reveals His Secret with His Own Tongue
The Stranger Fears the Thief

titleAr: توكلت في رزقي على الله خالقي
titleEn: I Have Placed My Trust for Provision in God My Creator

titleAr: لو كنت بالعقل تعطى ما تريد إذن
titleEn: If Intelligence Gave You What You Want

titleAr: علمي معي حيثما يممت فهو معي
titleEn: My Knowledge Is with Me Wherever I Go

titleAr: رام نفعا فضر من غير قصد
titleEn: He Sought Benefit but Caused Harm Unintentionally
Nothing Scratches Your Skin Like Your Own Nail
I Found That Contentment Is the Head of Wealth
It Is Misfortune That You Love
The True Scholar Is the Scholar by His Actions
Guard Your Soul and Carry It Upon What Adorns It
Every Time Life Disciplines Me
Seek Knowledge, for No One Is Born a Scholar
Wisdom Cannot Be Attained by One Whose Life
In Proportion to Effort You Attain Heights
If We Favor Ali, They Call Us Rejectors

titleAr: يا آل بيت رسول الله حبكم
titleEn: O Family of the Messenger of God, Your Love
I Treated All People with Tact, but My Envier
I Found That the Companion of Knowledge Is Noble
Three Things Are the Destroyers of Mankind
Shall I Scatter Pearls Among Grazing Cattle
Be Chaste, and Your Women Will Be Chaste
I Give Generously from What I Have
I Have Tested You and Tried My Own Character
By the Station of My Humility Before Your Supreme Majesty
To You, God of Creation, I Raise My Plea
Knowledge, by Its Grace, for the One Who Serves It
I Am Content with My Daily Sustenance
Guard Your Tongue, O Human Being

titleAr: ما شئت كان ، وإن لم أشأ
titleEn: What You Will Happens, Even If I Do Not Will It

titleAr: إذا رمت أن تحيا سليما من الردى
titleEn: If You Wish to Live Safe from Ruin
Indeed God Has Perceptive Servants
Weigh Those Who Weigh You
Some Eyes Stay Awake While Others Sleep
I Killed My Ambitions and Gave My Soul Rest
I See You Branding Me with the Mark of Your Favor
Do Not Bear the Burden of One Who Reminds You of Favors

titleAr: إني معزيك لا أني على ثقة
titleEn: I Offer You Condolence, Not Because I Am Certain
All Sciences Besides the Quran Are a Distraction
When Your Winds Blow Favorably, Seize the Moment
No One Can Attain All Knowledge
The Fool's Standing with the Scholar
When in a Gathering We Mention Ali
Turn Away from the Ignorant Fool

titleAr: وعين الرضا عن كل عيب كليلة
titleEn: The Eye of Approval Is Blind to Every Fault

titleAr: أرى حمرا ترعى وتعلف ما تهوى
titleEn: I See Donkeys Grazing and Fed What They Desire
The Mu'allaqa of Imru' al-Qays
Description of the Horse
Description of the Night
Description of Lightning and Rain
Memory of the Beloved
Will he slay me while my sword lies beside me

titleAr: ذهبتَ في الهجران في غير مذهبِ
titleEn: You departed in estrangement without cause
We are hurried toward a hidden decree
O Hind, do not marry a weakling
Rain watered the watering-places and Lal'a

titleAr: الخّيرُ ما طَلَعَتْ شَمسٌ ومَا غَرَبَتْ
titleEn: Good is sought as long as the sun rises
I witness the wild raid bearing me forward

titleAr: ألا يا لهف هِنْدٍ إثْرَ قَوْمٍ
titleEn: Alas for Hind's grief after those people
Our neighbor, for calamities keep returning

titleAr: يَا بُؤسَ لِلقَلْبِ بَعْد اليَوْمِ ما آبَهْ
titleEn: Woe to the heart for what it returned to
I visited the tribe's dwelling at dawn
Your night at Athmad has grown long

titleAr: ألا أبْلِغْ بَني حُجْرِ بنِ عَمْرٍو؛
titleEn: Convey to Banu Hujr ibn Amr my message
Did you remind yourself of what will not return
How wondrous Zubdan became smooth flat ground
I see my camels, praise God, have grown heavy
I saddled the white camels and urged them on
I drive away poems from me as one drives cattle

titleAr: سَمَا لَكَ شوْقٌ بَعدَما كان أقصَرَا
titleEn: Longing rose in you after it had subsided

titleAr: لَعَمْرُكَ ما قَلْبي إلى أهْلِهِ بِحُرْ
titleEn: By your life, my heart does not return freely to its people
Many an archer from Banu Tha'al
The sons of 'Awf built a noble lineage
A steady downpour with gentle drizzle in it

titleAr: لَنِعمَ الفَتى تَعشُو إلى ضَوْءِ نَارِهِ
titleEn: How fine the young man whose fire one seeks in darkness
O Harir Amr, I am as though intoxicated

titleAr: مَنَعتَ اللّيثَ من أكلِ ابنِ حُجْرِ
titleEn: You prevented the lion from devouring Ibn Hujr
Convey to Banu Zayd what I have met from them

titleAr: أمَاوِيَّ! هَلْ لي عِنْدَكُم من مُعرَّسِ
titleEn: O Mawiyya, is there a stopping place with you for me
Pause at the old campsite of As'as
What dead creature rises through its death
Whose Is This Ancient Ruin?

titleAr: أمِنْ ذِكرِ سلمَى أنْ نأتْكَ تَنوصُ
titleEn: Does the Memory of Salma Drive You Restlessly Away?
Help Me Watch This Flashing Lightning

titleAr: جَزِعتُ ولم أجزَعْ مِنَ البَينِ مَجْزَعا
titleEn: I Grieved at Parting, Yet Did Not Truly Grieve

titleAr: لَعَمري لَقَدْ بَانَتْ بحَاجَةِ ذِي الهَوَى
titleEn: By My Life, She Departed with the Longing of a Lover
He Dwelt at the Oasis Inside Busra

titleAr: ألا عِمْ صَبَاحًا أيّهَا الرَّبْعُ وَانْطِقِ
titleEn: Good Morning, O Ruined Campsite — Speak!
Do Not Abandon Me, O Rabi'

titleAr: ألا عِمْ صَبَاحًا أيّهَا الطّلَلُ البَالي
titleEn: Good Morning, O Crumbling Ruin

titleAr: دَعْ عَنكَ نَهبًا صِيحَ في حَجَرَاتِهِ
titleEn: Leave Aside the Plunder Cried Out in Its Quarters
O Dwelling of Mawiyya at Ha'il

titleAr: يا لَهْفَ هِنْدٍ إذ خَطِئنَ كاهِلًا
titleEn: Alas, O Hind, That They Missed Kahil
Greet the Departed Camel-Loads by the Side of al-'Azl
O Tha'al! How Far From Me Are the Sons of Tha'al!
I Have Set Down My Saddle Among Banu Tha'al

titleAr: مَنْ كان يأمُلُ عَقْرَ داريَ مِنْ
titleEn: Whoever Hopes to Destroy My Home
As If Wine and the Rain's Descent
I Was Exchanged From Wail and Kinda for 'Adwan
Did I Not Tell You That Time Is a Ghoul?
Your Eyes — Their Tears Overflow in Buckets
War, When It First Begins, Is a Young Girl
A Mail-Clad Warrior Whose Chest I Laid Open with the Spear
Whose Are These Dwellings I Passed at Suham?
May God Curse the Barajim Altogether
As If When I Descended on al-Mu'alla
News Came to Me and My Companions on a Hilltop
How Have Your Reproaches Found a Path Against Me?
When She Saw That the Watering Place Was Her Only Aim
Whose Ruin Did I See That Stirred My Grief?

titleAr: قِفا نَبكِ من ذكرَى حَبيبٍ وَعِرْفانِ
titleEn: Stop — Let Us Weep for a Beloved and Her Trace

titleAr: أفْسَدتَ بالمَنّ ما أوْلَيتَ من شِعَمٍ
titleEn: Generosity Ruined by Boasting

titleAr: ألا يَا عَينِ! بكّي لي شَنِينَا
titleEn: Weep for the Fallen Kings
The Radiant Rudayni Spear

titleAr: ومَا هَاجَ هذا الشَّوْقَ غَيْرُ مَنَازِلٍ
titleEn: Ruined Dwellings Stirring Longing

titleAr: ومَا كُنْتُ أخْشَى أنْ أبِيتَ بِحِمْيَرٍ
titleEn: Exile, Gazelles, and Pastoral Plenty
Satire Against al-Farazdaq

titleAr: في مدح عبد الملك بن مروان
titleEn: In Praise of Abd al-Malik ibn Marwan
Su'ad Has Departed
Satire Against al-Akhtal
On Glory and Valor

titleAr: ألا بكرت عرسي توائم من لحى
titleEn: My wife rose early to reproach me
Why did you not ask, not being ignorant
If death or old age overtakes you
From a desolate ruin worn by time

titleAr: ما برح الرسم الذي بين حنجر
titleEn: The ruin between Hanjir still remains
We struck at dawn the clan of Banu Jihash

titleAr: أبت ذكرة من حب ليلى تعودني
titleEn: The memory of Layla's love returns to haunt me
My wife has finally given me warning

titleAr: لو كنت أعجب من شيء لأعجبني
titleEn: Were I to marvel at anything, this would amaze me
Pause at the campsite of Dhat al-Mazahir
By your life, but for God's mercy I would
I journeyed to my people to call most of them

titleAr: بان الشباب وأمسى الشيب قد أزفا
titleEn: Youth has departed and old age has drawn near
How did the phantom come to haunt you, circling
The day of Wajj expelled the people of Hablaqa
From Nawar's campsite you recognized the worn abode

titleAr: أعلم أني متى ما يأتني قدري
titleEn: I know that whenever my fate comes to me
Old age has expelled the dark hair from his temples
Convey from me to Bujayr a message
Asma' has cut the bonds between us
My wife rose early to blame and reproach me
Su'ad has gone, and my heart today is stricken
From Umm Shaddad the ruins of the campsites
Do you recognize a ruin between Rahman and al-Raqm

titleAr: يقول حياي من عوف ومن جشم
titleEn: My kinfolk from Awf and Jusham say to me
A blazing noon when its gazelles cannot rest

titleAr: تقول ابنتي ألهى أبي حب أرضه
titleEn: My daughter says: my father is distracted by love of his land
From the ruin of the abode desolate for years
Come to us, clan of Bahtha — for the land
She came to me early at dawn to rebuke me
Truly Jua Fulfilled Her Oath
A Dishevelled Man Loose-Shouldered I Roused
For What Time Does a Man Hoard His Benefit
Know O Messenger of God You Shall Reach Me
The Prophet Wiped His Brow

titleAr: لاتفش سرك إلا عند ذي ثقة
titleEn: Reveal Not Your Secret Except to the Trustworthy

titleAr: هل حبل رملة قبل البين مبتور
titleEn: Is Ramla's Rope Cut Before Departure
A Night of Longing Whose Stars Seemed Scattered

titleAr: كأن امرأ لم يلق عيشا بنعمة
titleEn: As Though a Man Had Never Enjoyed a Life of Ease
Silent and Eloquent His Silence Is of Wisdom
There Is No Hope for One Who Rides Peril
Do You Expect My Apology O Son of Arwa
The Hunters Surrounded Their Prey and Behold

titleAr: له عنق تلوي بما وصلت به
titleEn: She Has a Neck That Twists with Its Ornaments
By Your Life I Did Not Fear for My Father
Sons of Hurthan bore the fledgling
Usayma' circled the camels and stirred

titleAr: أصْبَحْتُ أمْشي بَعْدَ سَلْمى بن مالكٍ
titleEn: I walk on after Salma son of Malik

titleAr: أرَى النّفسَ لَجّتْ في رَجاءٍ مُكذِّبِ
titleEn: I see the soul persisting in false hope

titleAr: قَضِّ اللُّبانَة َ لا أبَا لكَ واذْهَبِ
titleEn: Fulfill your purpose and go, fatherless one
The heart grew restless, would it had not
We spent the night where evening found us
I praised God, and God is the Praised
Affairs are settled and the promise fulfilled

titleAr: ما إنْ تُعَرّي المَنُونُ مِنْ أحَدِ
titleEn: Death strips no one bare of its reach
You two shall never exhaust Arbad's goodness
The tribe departed from Hajar after the early start

titleAr: وَلمْ تَحْمَ عَبدُ اللّهِ، لا درَّ دَرُّها،
titleEn: Banu Abdallah did not defend, curse them

titleAr: يا بشْرُ بشرَ بَني إيَادٍ أيُّكُمْ
titleEn: O Bishr, bearer of tidings of Iyad
Whoever among you was ignorant of me

titleAr: أعاذِلَ قُومي فاعذُلي الآنَ أوْ ذَري
titleEn: O blame me now or leave it, blamer
By my life if the informant spoke truly
Every fierce adversary reminds me of Arbad
I weep for Abu al-Hazzaz at the gathering
Only the righteous truly guard piety
My daughters wished their father would live on

titleAr: دَعي اللَّوْمَ أوْ بِيني كشِقِّ صَديعِ
titleEn: Leave off blame or depart like a split crack
We waste away while the rising stars do not

titleAr: يا مَيَّ قُومي في المَآتِمِ وَانْدُبي
titleEn: O Mayy, rise up in the mourning gatherings
I came to Abu Hind with Hind and Malik

titleAr: رَأيتَ ابنَ بَدرٍ ذُلَّ قومِكَ فاعْترِفْ
titleEn: You saw ibn Badr humble your people, confess it
Did you not pass by the ancient ruins
Kubaysha settled at 'Aqil after your time
Whose ruins does Uthal embrace
The ruins told nothing of their people
To God Belongs the Greatest Grace
Convey My Message to Banu Kilab
Rise When the Carefree Sleep, Lament Awf
Ask Man What He Truly Seeks
Piety Is the Finest Gain
We Come to You, Best of All Creation
Old Ruins of Khawla at al-Rusays
Deserted and Bare Are Wasit and Baram
I Said to My Companions at Dhat Ghisl
The Encampments Are Erased, Their Halting Place and Their Abode
For Hind at the Heights of al-Agarr Are Ruins
She Saw That I Had Grown Pale and Lean
Foolishly You Blamed Me, Speaking Without Blame
When News of Tufayl and His Clan Reached Me
Our Land Wept When We Departed

titleAr: عفَا الرَّسْمُ أمْ لا، بَعْدَ حَوْلٍ تَجَرَّمَا
titleEn: Erased or Not, a Full Year Has Passed
When Amir Called Me to Revile Them
Gone Are the Guardian and the Defender
The Ruins at Muta'li' and Aban Have Faded
I Visited the Tribe's Abodes at al-Sab'an
If Your People Have Wronged, O Yazid
I Hear That Abu Hanif Has Blamed Me
I Pass by the Dwellings of Layla
My Heart Clung to an Arab Girl
O Doves of the Arak Trees
O Would That the Days of Youth

titleAr: فَلَوْ أَنَّ مَا بِي مِنْ جَوًى
titleEn: If What Burns Within Me

titleAr: أَنَا الَّذِي نَظَرَ الأَعْمَى إِلَى أَدَبِي
titleEn: I Am He Whose Literature the Blind Can See
According to the Measure of Resolve
If You Venture for Noble Glory
O the Burning of My Heart

titleAr: لَا يَحْمِلُ الحِقْدَ مَنْ تَعْلُو بِهِ الرُّتَبُ
titleEn: He Who Rises Does Not Bear Grudges
Do You Deny My Brotherhood?
Do the Watchers Spy on Your Night Visit?
They Have Attributed Affliction to the Tents
Am I a Laughingstock Like the Samaritan?
The Heart Knows Its Own Malady Best
The Blame of Blamers Around My Lost Heart
Congratulations Are for Equals
Let Every Swaggering Walker Be a Ransom

titleAr: ولا عيب فيهم غير أن سيوفهم
titleEn: Their Only Flaw Is That Their Swords Are Notched
I Saw You Bestow Generously upon Poets
I Wrestle Longing for You, but Longing Prevails
The Finest Dye for Iron
Does He Who Troubles You Know What Ails You?
A Black One Whose Heart Is Narrow
Let My Beloved Ones Fill the Cups
Two Assemblies Distinguished Between Them
The Clouds Presented Themselves as We Returned
Perfume Is Something I No Longer Need

titleAr: ألا ما لسيف الدولة اليوم عاتبا
titleEn: Why Is Sayf al-Dawla Displeased Today?

titleAr: لما نسبت فكنت ابنا لغير أب
titleEn: When You Were Attributed, You Were Son of No Father

titleAr: يا أخت خير أخ يا بنت خير أب
titleEn: O Sister of the Best Brother, O Daughter of the Best Father
The Rat Who Sought Refuge Is Now Captured
I Reproach You So You May Make Amends
The People Did Not Treat Dabba Fairly
May the Branded Steeds Be Your Ransom
I See a Keen Blade That Dazzles the Polishers
Aid with Your Generosity Words I Left Behind
A Flock Whose Beauties I Was Denied
This Day After Tomorrow Bears a Fragrance
A Maiden Whose Body Has No Soul
With Your Faintest Smile, Minds Come Alive
Night Battles Me Fiercely Over You
O Stirrer of Every Soaring Noble Deed
The Rhymes Did Not Slander You
Today Is Your Promise — Where Is the Appointed Time?
I Parted from You

titleAr: عيد بأية حال عدت يا عيد
titleEn: O Eid, in What State Have You Returned?
O You Who Saw the Wise Man Betrayed

titleAr: ما الشوق مقتنعا مني بذا الكمد
titleEn: Longing Is Not Content with This Anguish of Mine
What Farewell Is This — The Farewell of a Grieving Lover
How Many Slain Like Me, a Martyr

titleAr: وشادن روح من يهواه في يده
titleEn: A Fawn Whose Lover's Soul Is in His Hand
Is This Musawir or the Sun's Horn?
By Hope in Your Generosity, Poverty Is Driven Away
Your Pleasure Is the Pleasure I Prefer
The Prince — May God Prolong His Reign
Fasting and Feasting, Festivals and Ages

titleAr: ظلم لذا اليوم وصف قبل رؤيته
titleEn: To Describe This Day Before Seeing It Is Injustice
I Know Full Well — and the Wise Man Is Knowing
He Got from Me What I Got from Him
Leaving Your Praise Is Like Satire Against Myself

titleAr: زعمت أنك تنفي الظن عن أدبي
titleEn: You Claimed You Would Dispel Doubt About My Art

titleAr: باد هواك صبرت أم لم تصبرا
titleEn: Your Love Is Plain, Whether You Endured or Not
I See That Closeness Has Turned to Estrangement
The Clear Wine Passed to You, O Son of Ibrahim
Remnants of a People Who Foretold Their Own Ruin

titleAr: لا تنكرن رحيلي عنك في عجل
titleEn: Do Not Blame My Hasty Departure
My Excuse from Virgin Affairs
Easy Now, O Plain Watered by Showers
You Command Seclusion Behind a Veil
Do Not Blame the One Who Sees the Sun
Like My Sword's Watered Pattern
An Ear That Reminded No One
Sweeter Than Aged Wine
Standing Upon Heads Is Little for Him
More Foolish Than a Slave and His Wife
She Appeared and Stirred a Fever
My Night in Damascus on a Bed of Fire

titleAr: إذا اعتل سيف الدولة اعتلت الأرض
titleEn: When Sayf al-Dawla Falls Ill, the Earth Falls Ill

titleAr: مضى الليل والفضل الذي لك لا يمضي
titleEn: The Night Has Passed but Your Virtue Endures
You Have Done to Us What the Sky Does to Its Earth
The Last Breath of a Soul Bidding Farewell
Grief Agitates and Composure Restrains
By My Father, We Loved and Parted
With It and Its Like He Split the Ranks
A Messenger Claiming Kinship with My Beloved

titleAr: موقع الخيل من نداك طفيف موقع الخيل من نداك طفيف
titleEn: Horses Are a Trifle Beside Your Generosity
I Have Prepared Swords for the Treacherous
How Light Is Long Imprisonment and Ruin
Sleeplessness Upon Sleeplessness
What Station Shall I Ascend?

titleAr: لعينيك ما يلقى الفؤاد وما لقي
titleEn: For Your Eyes Is What the Heart Endures
A Braided One Without Flaw
What Ails the Green Meadows and Gardens?
If He Excelled in Praising Her
I Have Not Seen a Companion but You

titleAr: فدى لك من يقصر عن مداكا
titleEn: May All Who Fall Short of You Be Your Ransom

titleAr: قد بلغت الذي أردت من البر
titleEn: You Have Achieved the Piety You Sought
Ignorance Killed You Before Your Death

titleAr: لا خيل عندك تهديها ولا مال
titleEn: You Have No Horses to Gift, Nor Wealth
I Would Ransom — What Pleases the Messenger?

titleAr: لك يا منازل في القلوب منازل
titleEn: You, O Dwellings, Hold Dwellings in Our Hearts
O You Who Humbles the Lion with His Whip

titleAr: أحيا وأيسر ما قاسيت ما قتلا
titleEn: I Live, Though the Least of What I Suffered Would Have Killed

titleAr: إذا ما شربت الخمر صرفا مهنأ
titleEn: When You Drink Wine Pure and Pleasant
O the Burning of My Heart for One Whose Heart Is Cold
Is There No Generous Soul in This World?
Does the Wind Pass Gently at My Command?

titleAr: على قدر أهل العزم تأتي العزائم
titleEn: Great Deeds Come to Match the Greatness of Men

titleAr: بم التعلل لا أهل ولا وطن
titleEn: With What Shall I Console Myself? No Family, No Homeland
People Before Us Kept Company with This Age

titleAr: أبلى الهوى أسفا يوم النوى بدني
titleEn: Love Has Worn My Body with Sorrow on the Day of Parting
I Concealed My Love Even from You, Out of Dignity
Sound Judgment Comes Before the Courage of the Brave
People Are Alike Until They See You
If Tayy Were Indeed Base
Alas — a Substitute for My Sighing 'Ah!'

titleAr: كفى بك داء أن ترى الموت شافيا
titleEn: It Is Illness Enough That You See Death as a Cure

titleAr: أريك الرضى لو أخفت النفس خافيا
